phase_legend.P1: Cataloged from code (user story + expected behaviour written)
phase_legend.P2: Tested against expected behaviour; result + severity recorded

features.user_story: As a reader on any page, I want to click the DX logo/wordmark to return home, so I can always get back to the index.
features.expected: Masthead .brand-block links to '/'. Logo img + brand-word render; aria-label identifies it as home. Present on home, about, article, coming-soon, 404, phase0.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the masthead label to reflect where I am (Words / About / 404 / Phase 0), so I have lightweight orientation.
features.expected: brand-word reads 'Words' on home/article/coming-soon, 'About' on about (actually 'Words'), '404' on 404, 'Phase 0' on phase0. (Note: about.astro uses 'Words'.)
features.p3: NOTE: about brand-word='Words' by design — fine

features.user_story: As a reader, I want to toggle dark/light mode and have my choice remembered across pages and visits, so the site matches my environment.
features.expected: Click #modeToggle flips html.dark-mode; writes dxmode cookie (max-age 1yr, SameSite=Lax). On load: cookie wins, else prefers-color-scheme, else light. No FOUC (head-phase sets class pre-paint). Button label + aria-pressed update. Logo swaps logo-dark/logo-white via data-base. theme-color + color-scheme synced. Follows OS change only when no explicit cookie.

features.user_story: As a keyboard/screen-reader user, I want a 'skip to content' link, so I can bypass the masthead.
features.expected: First focusable element is a .skip-link anchoring to the page's main region (#archive / #article / #about / #main / #phase0). Visible on focus.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the editorial ruler accents (top/left) to render as decoration without interfering with assistive tech.
features.expected: .ruler-top / .ruler-left present and aria-hidden; purely decorative, no focus/AT exposure.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want footer links to Instagram, X, Feed (and About on home), so I can follow or subscribe from any page.
features.expected: Footer renders Instagram + X (target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer), Feed (/feed.xml). Home footer also has About. 'Built by Dex · Est. 2026'.

features.user_story: As a feed-reader user, I want autodiscovery of the Atom feed, so my reader finds it automatically.
features.expected: <link rel=alternate type=application/atom+xml href=/feed.xml> present in <head> on home, about, phase0. (Articles/coming-soon/404 do NOT include it — verify intent.)
features.p3: FIX: Added <link rel=alternate atom> feed autodiscovery to ArticleLayout + ComingSoonLayout heads
features.p4: PASS — feed link present on article + coming-soon

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a proper favicon and iOS touch icon, so the site looks polished in tabs and home screen.
features.expected: favicon-32.png + apple-touch-icon.png linked on every page; files exist in /img.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want fonts to load fast and consistently without third-party calls, so type renders quickly under strict CSP.
features.expected: Anton + IBM Plex Sans (Regular/Bold/Italic) self-hosted in /fonts, preloaded with crossorigin; no Google Fonts. font-src 'self'.

features.user_story: As a mobile reader, I want the layout to adapt to small screens with safe-area support, so reading is comfortable on a phone.
features.expected: viewport meta width=device-width, viewport-fit=cover on all pages; CSS responsive breakpoints (plates, article, phase0) collapse multi-col grids on narrow widths.
features.p3: NOTE: mobile 375 verified visually

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want each page to declare a unique title, description, and canonical URL, so listings are correct and duplicates collapse.
features.expected: Unique <title> + meta description per page. Canonical absolute URL with trailing slash for home/about/articles; 404 canonical is /404 (noindex).

features.user_story: As someone sharing a link, I want a rich preview (image, title, description), so shares look intentional.
features.expected: og:title/description/image(1200x630)/url/type/site_name + twitter:card summary_large_image on home/about/articles/coming-soon/phase0. og image files exist per slug.
features.p3: NOTE: all og-<slug>.png exist

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want structured data identifying the site and author, so rich results can attach.
features.expected: Home emits @graph with WebSite (#website) + Person (#person) incl sameAs Instagram/X, jobTitle, worksFor TPH. Valid JSON-LD.

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want the about page marked as a ProfilePage about the Person, so identity is unambiguous.
features.expected: about.astro emits ProfilePage→mainEntity Person (#person) with name, url, jobTitle, sameAs, description. Valid JSON-LD.

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want each essay marked as a BlogPosting with dates, author, image, wordCount, so it can earn article rich results.
features.expected: ArticleLayout emits BlogPosting with headline, description, datePublished/dateModified (ISO), author/publisher Person, image (og), wordCount. Valid JSON-LD.

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want the Phase 0 landing marked as a WebPage tied to the site/person, so it is contextualized.
features.expected: phase0 emits @graph WebPage (#webpage) isPartOf #website + author #person. Valid JSON-LD.

features.user_story: As a crawler, I want robots.txt to allow crawling and point to the sitemap, so I can index efficiently.
features.expected: robots.txt: User-agent * / Allow: / ; Sitemap: https://imjustdex.com/sitemap-index.xml.

features.user_story: As a crawler, I want a sitemap listing only publicly-live URLs with sensible priorities, so noindex/coming-soon pages don't muddy coverage.
features.expected: @astrojs/sitemap builds sitemap-index.xml + sitemap-0.xml. /words/* included only if publicly-live (status published AND date<=now). 404 excluded. brand/ + phase0/ injected as customPages. Home priority 1.0, about 0.7, phase0 0.9, brand 0.5, articles 0.8.
features.p3: NOTE: 18 URLs, 404 excluded, coming-soon excluded

features.user_story: As a site owner, I want unpublished and not-found pages excluded from the index, so only chosen content ranks.
features.expected: ComingSoonLayout + 404 set <meta name=robots content=noindex>. 404 also nofollow.

features.user_story: As a returning reader, I want a masthead 'issue' line showing entry number, the current week, and the latest essay, so I instantly see what's newest.
features.expected: LatestBar shows 'Entry No. NN' (NN = count of published essays, zero-padded), 'Week of <leadDate>', and a 'Latest' link to newest essay with lane·date·readTime. Links to /words/<slug>/.
features.p3: NOTE: Entry No.14, Wk Jun14

features.user_story: As a first-time visitor, I want a hero introducing Dexter with the tagline and quick About/Subscribe links, so I understand whose site this is.
features.expected: IdentityHero renders name 'Dexter Jakes', sub 'ImJustDex', tagline, bio, 'Read the About' → /about/, 'Subscribe' → #subscribe-manifesto.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want to see how many essays exist per lane (Faith/Identity/Art), with empty lanes shown as queued, so I know the shape of the archive.
features.expected: LaneIndex shows each lane with zero-padded count + 'essay/essays'. Lanes with 0 render as non-interactive .lane-cell-queued ('In the queue', aria-disabled). Multi-lane essays count in each lane.
features.p3: NOTE: queued state not live-exercised (all lanes have essays); code verified

features.user_story: As a reader, I want to click a lane to filter the archive to that lane and click again to clear, so I can browse by theme.
features.expected: lane-filter.js: clicking Faith/Identity sets aria-pressed=true, hides non-matching <article> via hidden, sets data-filter on .plates (CSS suppresses row-heads), announces count via live region. Clicking active clears. Queued (Art) is non-interactive. No-JS = all visible.
features.p3: FIX: lane-filter.js: clear() now restores each article's base hidden state (respects earlier-toggle); hides View-Earlier wrap while filtered
features.p4: PASS — fresh 8 → filter 11 (wrap hidden) → clear 8, button stays collapsed; earlier toggle still works after cycle

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the newest essay shown as a large lead plate, so the freshest piece is unmistakable.
features.expected: LeadPlate renders newest published essay as .plate-lead with Latest badge, lane, date, readTime, title, deck, Read→ CTA; links to /words/<slug>/.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want essays from the latest essay's calendar month grouped together, so recent cadence is visible.
features.expected: Essays in same UTC year+month as lead (excluding lead) render under RowHead '02 This Month'. Hidden entirely if none.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want older essays grouped under 'Earlier', so the full archive is browsable.
features.expected: Essays older than lead's month render under RowHead 'Earlier' (num 03 if This Month exists else 02). First 6 visible, rest hidden (.earlier-more[hidden]).
features.p3: NOTE: 8 visible / 6 hidden correct

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a button to reveal the remaining earlier essays in place and collapse them again, so I'm not overwhelmed by default.
features.expected: earlier-toggle.js: button shows '— N more', label 'View Earlier Essays/Essay' (correct plural). Click reveals .earlier-more (removes hidden), sets aria-expanded, label→'Show Less', count→'showing all'. Re-click hides. No-JS: button inert, overflow stays hidden.
features.p3: NOTE: plural + expand/collapse correct in isolation

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the archive plates to tile cleanly into full rows of the 12-col grid, so the grid never looks broken.
features.expected: packCascade/packSegment assign wide(5)+secondary(3)+banner(4) rotations; trailing 1→row(12), 2→half+half(6+6). Editor plate.row:true forces a full 12-col row and re-segments around it.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want textured and plain plates to alternate so cards contrast their neighbors (DESIGN-SYSTEM §6).
features.expected: coverMap walks This Month+Earlier reading order, alternating plain/textured; frontmatter plate.image is an override; auto-slots cycle concrete/lines/noise. Opens on a plain plate (lead carries texture above).

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a preview of the next upcoming essay with a notify CTA, so I can anticipate it.
features.expected: If an upcoming essay exists, IssueNext renders earliest upcoming with 'Next' tag, title, meta, 'Notify me →', linking to its coming-soon URL. Hidden when no upcoming essays.
features.p3: NOTE: correctly absent (no upcoming)

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a clear subscribe pitch with the value props and an email form, so I can join the list.
features.expected: SubscribeManifesto renders kicker, title, promise, 3 value-prop list items, email form (#subscribe-manifesto anchor target), 'No spam, ever'. Honeypot field hidden.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the subscribe form to confirm inline without a page reload, including 'already subscribed' and error states, so I get clear feedback.
features.expected: subscribe.js intercepts .sm-form submit, validates, POSTs to /api/subscribe, then: success→'You're in.' confirm; already_subscribed→'Already on the list.'; invalid_email/rate_limited/generic→inline .sm-error. Button shows 'Sending…' while loading.
features.p3: NOTE: shows error on 500, no false success

features.user_story: As a reader, I want each essay headed by its lanes/tags, date, read time, title and deck, so I know what I'm about to read.
features.expected: ArticleLayout header lists lanes then tags as .article-tag chips joined by ' + ' separator (CSS ::before), formatted date <time>, read time, h1 title, deck.
features.p3: FIX: Multi-tag ' + ' separator lived in an Astro <style> block that the build inlined; strict style-src 'self' CSP blocked it in production. Fixed via build.inlineStylesheets:'never' (external hashed CSS).
features.p4: PASS — ::before content ' + ' now applied (rgb verified) via /_astro CSS, CSP-clean

features.user_story: As a reader, I want the essay body to render with all its editorial components, so the long-form reads as designed.
features.expected: MDX Content renders inside .article-body for live essays. All 14 essays render without error.
features.p3: NOTE: all 14 build + render

features.user_story: As a long-form reader, I want a progress bar showing how far I've scrolled, so I gauge remaining length.
features.expected: progress.js sizes .reading-progress width 0-100% on scroll (rAF-throttled), updates aria-valuenow. Initializes progressbar ARIA. No-ops if elements missing.
features.p3: NOTE: code+elements verified; live scroll not exercisable (headless rAF/scroll throttled)

features.user_story: As a long-form reader, I want a floating label of the current section, so I keep my place.
features.expected: progress.js tracks .section-head h2 landmarks; shows .section-indicator with current section name when 2%<pct<98% and a head is above 40% viewport; hides otherwise. All essays use SectionHead.
features.p3: NOTE: 5 section-heads present; code verified; live scroll not exercisable

features.user_story: As a reader, I want to share the essay to X with title prefilled, so sharing is one click.
features.expected: Share link href = x.com/intent/tweet?url=<encoded article url>&text=<title — DX>, target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a 'Copy link' button that copies the URL and confirms, so I can paste it anywhere.
features.expected: essay.js: click copies data-copy-link via navigator.clipboard (fallback execCommand). Button label→'Copied' 2s; SR live region announces. Present on articles + about.
features.p3: NOTE: handler+SR announce OK; clipboard blocked only in headless

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a Follow Me link to Instagram in the share bar, so I can follow after reading.
features.expected: share bar 'Follow Me' → instagram.com/ImJustDex, target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer. Present on articles + about.

features.user_story: As a reader at the end of an essay, I want to subscribe inline and get confirmation, so I don't leave to join the list.
features.expected: signup.js intercepts .email-signup submit, validates email, POSTs to /api/subscribe (source=article), then collapses to 'You're in. Check your inbox.' Honeypot hidden. Should reflect server failure, not blanket success.
features.p3: FIX: signup.js now parses JSON, checks res.ok && data.ok; distinct success / already-subscribed / error states; re-enables submit on error
features.p4: PASS — server 500 now shows 'Something broke…', button re-enabled, no false success

features.user_story: As a reader, I want prev/next links between essays, with upcoming ones clearly marked as teasers, so I can move through the series.
features.expected: article-nav renders prev (rel=prev) and next. If next.ghost: rendered as .article-nav-ghost teaser with optional date, NO rel=next. If next not ghost: rel=next link. Ghost flags must be accurate (essay still upcoming).
features.p3: FIX: ArticleLayout derives effective nextGhost from the target essay's real live status (getCollection + isPubliclyLive); stale frontmatter ghost flags ignored once target is live
features.p4: PASS — pulpit/should-have-killed-it/not-the-same-as-alive render normal next + rel=next; ghost STILL shows when target genuinely upcoming (verified by future-dating)

features.user_story: As a search engine, I want rel=prev/next hints in the head, so sequential essays are understood.
features.expected: Head includes <link rel=prev> if prev; <link rel=next> only if next AND not ghost.
features.p3: FIX: rel=next head link now driven by derived nextGhost (present when next is live)
features.p4: PASS — rel=next emitted for all 3 formerly-stale essays; suppressed only when target upcoming

features.user_story: As a reader, I want a '← Back' link, so I can return to the index after reading.
features.expected: '← Back' link to '/' at the bottom of articles, coming-soon, and about.

features.user_story: As a reader, I want pull quotes, scripture blocks, callouts, stat blocks, drop caps, closings and CTAs to render with editorial styling, so the essays feel designed.
features.expected: PullQuote, Scripture(+cite), ScriptureList, Callout, StatBlock, DropCap, Closing, SectionHead, ResearchCTA render their documented markup and are styled by article.css. Each renders without layout break in light + dark.
features.p3: FIX: ScriptureList scoped <style> was inlined by the build and CSP-blocked in production (refs/borders unstyled). Fixed via build.inlineStylesheets:'never'.
features.p4: PASS — .scripture-ref accent #cc0000 + uppercase + flex items now applied via external CSS

features.user_story: As a curious reader, I want collapsible study notes I can open for deeper references, so the main read stays clean.
features.expected: StudyNote uses native <details>/<summary>; clicking toggles open/closed; label + ref + body render; keyboard accessible by default.
features.p3: NOTE: details toggle open/close OK

features.user_story: As someone who clicked a teaser/social link for an unpublished essay, I want a real page showing when it publishes and a way to subscribe, so the link isn't a dead end.
features.expected: Upcoming essays (status published/upcoming + future date) build a ComingSoonLayout: 'Coming Soon' tag, long publish date, subscribe form, noindex, preserves OG. Flips to full article once date passes (build-time).
features.p3: FIX: Replaced 3 CSP-blocked inline style= attrs with .coming-tag/.coming-lead/.coming-note classes; styles now in external (Astro-hashed) CSS
features.p4: PASS (live) — coming-tag bg #cc0000 applied, 0 inline styles, no console CSP errors

features.user_story: As a reader, I want an About page telling Dexter's story with editorial styling, so I understand the person behind the writing.
features.expected: about.astro renders header (tag, h1, deck 'Est. 1994.'), body paragraphs incl intro, PullQuote, Closing; canonical /about/; ProfilePage JSON-LD.

features.user_story: As a reader on About, I want share (X / copy link / Follow Me), so I can share the page.
features.expected: share-bar with X intent (about url+text), Copy link button (data-copy-link about url), Follow Me → Instagram. essay.js wires copy.
features.p3: FIX: Added <span data-copy-status role=status aria-live> to About share bar (parity with articles)
features.p4: PASS — copy now announces to the live region

features.user_story: As a reader hitting a dead URL, I want a branded 404 explaining it and a way home, so I'm not stranded.
features.expected: 404.astro renders branded plate, headline, copy, 'Back to Home' → /. Served by Netlify with HTTP 404 on unmatched routes. noindex,nofollow.
features.p3: NOTE: HTTP 404 + branded plate + back-home + noindex,nofollow

features.user_story: As a feed-reader user, I want a valid Atom feed of published essays with lanes as categories, so I can subscribe in my reader.
features.expected: feed.xml.ts emits Atom: feed title/subtitle/links/id/updated/rights/author/icon/logo + one <entry> per publicly-live essay (title, link, id, published, updated, summary, author, <category> per lane with lane scheme). XML-escaped. Served application/xml.
features.p3: NOTE: 14 entries, 22 lane categories, valid Atom, escaped

features.user_story: As a human who opens the feed URL in a browser, I want a readable styled page, not raw XML, so the feed isn't intimidating.
features.expected: feed.xml references /feed.xsl via xml-stylesheet PI; browser applies XSL to render a human-readable feed; CSP relaxed (style-src unsafe-inline) for /feed.xml + /feed.xsl.
features.p3: NOTE: xsl referenced + 200 + relaxed CSP

features.user_story: As a ministry marketer, I want a long-form Phase 0 landing page with in-page anchor navigation, so I can read the pitch and jump to the signup.
features.expected: phase0/index.html renders 9 sections (Hero…Author); CTAs 'Get The Prompt' → #get, 'Read The System' → #definition; phases list; author block. Loads tokens+shell+phase0 CSS.
features.p3: NOTE: 10 sections, anchors

features.user_story: As a Phase 0 visitor, I want the signup form to validate, show success or a real error, and not lie on failure, so I trust the capture.
features.expected: phase0.js owns .phase0-capture (data-no-signup-js). On submit: validate (aria-invalid + error msg on fail), POST /api/subscribe (source=phase0), success→success state; failure→error copy + 'Retry'. Clears error on valid keystroke. noscript fallback shown without JS.
features.p3: NOTE: invalid->error+aria-invalid; 500->error+Retry (no false success)

features.user_story: As a visitor, I want the footer year to be current, so the page doesn't look stale.
features.expected: phase0.js sets #yr textContent to current year on load.
features.p3: NOTE: year 2026

features.user_story: As Dexter/collaborator, I want a living brand specimen at /brand/, so the design system is documented.
features.expected: /brand/ renders specimen (swatches, type, demos) with scoped CSP allowing style-src unsafe-inline; scripts extracted to brand.js. Linked in sitemap.
features.p3: NOTE: 200, 115KB, relaxed CSP

features.user_story: As the system, I want the subscribe endpoint to reject non-POST methods, so it isn't misused via GET.
features.expected: GET/other → 405 {ok:false,error:method_not_allowed}.
features.p3: NOTE: 405

features.user_story: As the system, I want to throttle repeated submissions per IP, so the form can't be abused.
features.expected: More than 5 POSTs/60s/IP → 429 {error:rate_limited}. In-memory sliding window keyed by client IP.
features.p3: NOTE: 429 after 5/min/IP

features.user_story: As the system, I want to silently drop bot submissions that fill the hidden field, so spam doesn't reach Mailchimp.
features.expected: If body.website is non-empty → return 200 {ok:true,message:subscribed} without calling Mailchimp.
features.p3: NOTE: honeypot->200 no Mailchimp

features.user_story: As the system, I want to reject malformed emails before calling Mailchimp, so we don't waste calls or store junk.
features.expected: Invalid EMAIL/email → 400 {error:invalid_email}. Accepts standard addresses.
features.p3: NOTE: 400 invalid_email

features.user_story: As a subscriber, I want my email added to the list, and a graceful 'already subscribed' if I'm on it, so I always get clear feedback.
features.expected: POST to Mailchimp members; success→200 {message:subscribed}; 400 'Member Exists'→200 {message:already_subscribed}; other upstream errors→502. Needs env vars (else 500 server_not_configured).
features.p3: NOTE: add+member-exists code verified; 500 path tested (no local creds)

features.user_story: As the site owner, I want to know where each subscriber came from (home/article/phase0), so I can attribute signups.
features.expected: merge_fields.SOURCE set (phase0→'phase0-landing'); a Mailchimp tag applied via tags endpoint (phase0→'phase-0', else source). Best-effort tag (subscriber added regardless).
features.p3: FIX: Added hidden source='coming-soon' field + aria-live to the coming-soon signup label
features.p4: PASS — source field present, attributed correctly

features.user_story: As a subscriber, I want graceful errors when the backend is misconfigured or Mailchimp is down, so I'm not left guessing.
features.expected: Missing env→500 server_not_configured; fetch throw→502 upstream_unreachable; non-ok upstream→502 upstream_error. JSON, Cache-Control no-store.
features.p3: FIX: Renamed function subscribe.js → subscribe.cjs (unambiguous CommonJS under package type:module); silences esbuild ESM warning
features.p4: PASS — endpoint works post-rename (405 / 400 invalid_email / 200 honeypot)

features.user_story: As a visitor on the old dxjakes.com, I want to be 301'd to imjustdex.com keeping my path, so old links still work and the brand is unified.
features.expected: https://dxjakes.com/* and https://www.dxjakes.com/* → https://imjustdex.com/:splat, 301, force.
features.p3: NOTE: config correct (absolute-URL host redirect); not locally testable

features.user_story: As a visitor, I want non-slash URLs to 301 to the slash canonical, so there's one canonical URL per page.
features.expected: /about, /phase0, and each /words/<slug> (no slash) → slashed form, 301, NOT forced (avoids loop). Astro trailingSlash:always.
features.p3: NOTE: config correct; netlify dev serves 200 via pretty-URL, prod 301s

features.user_story: As a visitor hitting the old /words archive, I want to land on home, so the single-archive design is honored.
features.expected: /words and /words/ → / , 301, force.
features.p3: NOTE: /words & /words/ -> / 301

features.user_story: As a visitor following an old TPH-prototype link, I want to land on home, so dead ops routes don't 404.
features.expected: /opsmeeting(/*), /leadership-prep(/*), /asana-workflow(/*) → / , 301, force.
features.p3: NOTE: ops/leadership/asana -> / 301

features.user_story: As the frontend, I want /api/subscribe to proxy same-origin to the Netlify function, so it clears the strict CSP connect-src 'self'.
features.expected: /api/subscribe → /.netlify/functions/subscribe, status 200 (rewrite, not redirect).
features.p3: NOTE: /api/subscribe -> function (200 rewrite)

features.user_story: As a visitor on an unknown URL, I want the branded 404 with a real 404 status, so it's correct for users and crawlers.
features.expected: /* → /404.html status 404 (no force) only when no real file matches.
features.p3: NOTE: unknown -> 404.html, HTTP 404

features.user_story: As a user, I want a strict CSP so the site can't load injected/3rd-party scripts, protecting me from XSS.
features.expected: Global CSP self-only (script/style/font/connect 'self'; img self+data+https; frame-ancestors none; object-src none; upgrade-insecure-requests). /brand/* + /feed.xml + /feed.xsl relax style-src to allow unsafe-inline. No inline scripts anywhere.
features.p3: FIX: No CSP change needed — kept strict style-src 'self'; build now emits external CSS (inlineStylesheets:'never') instead of CSP-violating inline <style>. Added /_astro/* immutable cache header.
features.p4: PASS — strict CSP intact; per-route overrides intact; component styles now external + applied

features.user_story: As a user, I want clickjacking/MIME/referrer/permissions protections + HSTS, so the site is hardened.
features.expected: X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy locked, HSTS 1yr preload, COOP/CORP same-origin on /*.
features.p3: NOTE: XFO/nosniff/referrer/permissions/HSTS/COOP/CORP all present

features.user_story: As a returning visitor, I want versioned assets cached immutably and HTML always fresh, so the site is fast but never stale.
features.expected: /css /js /fonts /img → max-age 31536000 immutable; HTML (*.html) → max-age 0 must-revalidate; /feed.xml → max-age 0 must-revalidate; fonts also ACAO *.
features.p3: NOTE: config correct; netlify dev forces max-age=0 locally, prod honors immutable

features.user_story: As the author, I want essays to flip from coming-soon to live automatically when their date passes, so I don't have to push code on publish day.
features.expected: isPubliclyLiveData: status==published AND date<=now. isUpcomingData: status in {published,upcoming} AND date>now. Single source shared by homepage, feed, sitemap, routes.
features.p3: NOTE: coming-soon build test confirmed flip + sitemap/feed exclusion

features.user_story: As the author, I want a daily scheduled rebuild, so date-gated essays go live without a manual deploy.
features.expected: GitHub Action cron 05:05 UTC + workflow_dispatch POSTs Netlify build hook (NETLIFY_BUILD_HOOK_URL secret); fails loudly if secret missing or non-2xx.
features.p3: NOTE: workflow YAML valid; cron not runnable locally

features.user_story: As the author, I want a tool to generate the 1200x630 OG share image per essay, so social previews stay on-brand.
features.expected: scripts/og-plate.mjs reads essay frontmatter, auto-numbers volume, renders via headless Chrome to img/og-<slug>.png. (Dev tool; not user-facing at runtime.)
features.p3: NOTE: node --check OK; render needs headless Chrome (dev tool, not runtime)

features.user_story: As a motion-sensitive user, I want animations/transitions suppressed when I set prefers-reduced-motion, so the site doesn't trigger discomfort.
features.expected: CSS honors @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) for animated elements (tokens.css, home-augment.css).
features.p3: NOTE: reduce: zeroes transition/animation durations + scroll-behavior auto

features.user_story: As a keyboard user, I want visible focus indicators on all interactive elements, so I can see where I am.
features.expected: Every CSS file defines :focus-visible styles; focus indicators visible in light + dark on links, buttons, form fields, toggles, plates.
features.p3: NOTE: focus-visible defined across all css files

features.user_story: As a low-vision reader, I want text/background contrast to meet WCAG AA in both modes, so copy is legible.
features.expected: Body, muted, faint, accent text meet >=4.5:1 (>=3:1 large) against their backgrounds in light AND dark mode.
features.p3: NOTE: computed AA: light body 12:1/muted 4.63:1/red 5.34:1; dark body 11.85:1/muted 5.13:1/red 6.2:1; large red 3.44:1>=3

features.user_story: As a screen-reader user, I want correct landmarks (header/nav/main/footer) and a sane heading order, so I can navigate structurally.
features.expected: Each page has header>nav[aria-label], main[id], footer[aria-label]; single h1; descending heading order; aria-labels on key sections.
features.p3: NOTE: header/nav/main/footer + single h1 per page
